sections.paragraph:
εὐσχημοσύνη, ης, ἡ (s. prec. two entries; Hippocr., Pla., X.+; Diod S 5, 32, 7; Epict. 4, 9, 11; OGI 339, 32; Michel 545, 8f [II b.c.]; 4 Macc 6:2; EpArist 284) the state of being appropriate for display, propriety, decorum, presentability, of clothing (cp. Maximus Tyr. 15, 3b; SIG 547, 37; 4 Macc 6:2) of modest concealment τὰ ἀσχήμονα ἡμῶν εὐ. περισσοτέραν ἔχει our unpresentable (parts) receive greater presentability (=are treated with greater modesty) 1 Cor 12:23.—M-M. TW. Spicq.

prec. prec. = preceding

Hippocr Hippocr = Corpus Hippocraticum, V–IV b.c.—List 5

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

Epict Epict , various works, I–II a.d.—List 5

OGI OGI = Orientis Graeci Inscriptiones Selectae—List 3

Michel Michel = CM., Recueil d’inscriptions grecques—List 3

EpArist EpArist = Epistle of Aristeas, II b.c.—List 5

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Maximus Tyr Maximus Tyr , II a.d.—List 5

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Spicq Spicq = CS., Lexique théologique du Nouveau Testament—Lists 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 414
